title: Cardiometabolic essentials
disclaimer: Educational content only - not medical advice. Consult your physician about your specific health situation.

lessons.title: The three signals: lipids, glucose, BP

blocks.markdown:
Three numbers do most of the work in cardiometabolic health:

- **Lipids** (cholesterol and triglycerides)
- **Glucose** (blood sugar regulation)
- **Blood pressure** (the force against artery walls)

They interact. Poor glucose control nudges lipids the wrong way; high blood pressure compounds the damage. You read them together, never in isolation.

cards.front: What are the three core cardiometabolic signals?
cards.back: Lipids, glucose, and blood pressure - read together, not in isolation.
They interact: poor glucose control shifts lipids unfavourably, and high BP compounds the vascular damage.

questions.stem: Which statement best reflects how these markers relate?
options: Each marker is independent of the others
They interact and should be read together
Only blood pressure matters for the heart
Glucose has no effect on lipids
questions.explanation: The markers interact - glucose dysregulation shifts lipids, and elevated blood pressure adds vascular strain. They're interpreted as a cluster.
